We perform a battery of additional analyses to assess the robustness of our
inference.

First, we re-estimate the baseline using an instrumental-variable design that
exploits geographic distance as the instrument. Second, we apply entropy
balancing to equate the treatment and control firms on observable covariates.
Third, we run a placebo test that assigns pseudo-treatment three years before the
actual event and find no effect. Fourth, we partition the sample by client size
and show the result concentrates among larger clients. Fifth, we add client
fixed effects to absorb time-invariant client heterogeneity.

Our inference is robust across these specifications.
